data.text: I’ve confirmed there isn’t an existing `artifacttypes` package yet, so I’m mapping the current config and workflow conventions first. Next I’m reading the bead-authoring template plus the code that already discovers plugin/workflow metadata so the new loader matches repository shape instead of inventing one.

data.output:
# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion

- **Epic beads** are aggregates by design. Criterion (c) (specific test names) and (d) (wired-in) commonly pass via children. The epic should still satisfy (a), (b), (e) — pointing at the children's collective `go test` set — and (f), (g), (h).
- **Doc-only beads** legitimately omit (c) (no tests) and (d) (no code path). They must still satisfy (e)'s lefthook clause and (h).
- **Pure deletion / rename beads** legitimately omit (d). They must still cite the deletion-target's file:line in (b) and assert byte-identical behavior preservation in (c).

---

## 7. References

- `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` (planned via `ddx-06b77652`) — P7 source.
- `.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md` — the audit that produced this template.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` — operational guidance on creating beads via `ddx bead create`.

cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.

Updated\\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\\n\\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\\n+\\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\\n+\\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\\n+\\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\\n+\\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\\n+\\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\\n+\\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \\\"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\\n+assigned\\\" reported on agent projects.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\\n+\\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\\n+\\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\\n+invocation**.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\\n+\\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\\n+```go\\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\\n+}\\n+```\\n+\\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\\n+events.\\n+\\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\\n+comment claims \\\"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\\n+termination\\\" but the actual behaviour is **\\\"2 hours of silence required to fire\\\"**.\\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\\n+timer indefinitely.\\n+\\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\\n+\\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\\n+\\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\\n+\\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\\n+\\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\\n+read indefinitely.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** consistent with \\\"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\\n+deadline\\\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\\n+against a live hung worker.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\\n+\\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\\n+\\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\\n+\\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\\n+as RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\\n+\\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop <id>` command that\\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\\n+plumbing). Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\\n+\\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\\n+\\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\\n+invocation**.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\\n+\\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\\n+```go\\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\\n+}\\n+```\\n+\\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\\n+events.\\n+\\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\\n+comment claims \\\"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\\n+termination\\\" but the actual behaviour is **\\\"2 hours of silence required to fire\\\"**.\\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\\n+timer indefinitely.\\n+\\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\\n+\\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\\n+\\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\\n+\\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\\n+\\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\\n+read indefinitely.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** consistent with \\\"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\\n+deadline\\\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:<port>/debug/pprof/goroutine`\\n+against a live hung worker.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\\n+\\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\\n+\\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\\n+\\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\\n+as RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 3. .ddx/executions/20260504T220127-177c17c0/manifest.json:573:          "body": "Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:\n\n- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or\n- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).\n\nThis is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\"\n\n### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)\n\n\"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\"\n\nEven with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). .ddx/executions/20260503T225413-91120fa0/manifest.json:574:          "body": "Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T230228-6c8b4237/prompt.md:607:+          "body": "Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.\n\nThe bead's \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.\n\n## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)\n\nCreate the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):\n\n### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing\nTitle: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)\nScope:\n- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).\n- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.\n- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.\n- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.\n- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.\n- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.\n- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.\nAcceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.\nEstimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.\n\n### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion\nTitle: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec\nScope:\n- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.\n- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.\n- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.\nAcceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.\nEstimated: ~300–500 LOC.\n\n### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)\nTitle: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit\nScope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:\n- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.\n- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.\n- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.\n- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).\n- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.\nAcceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.\nEstimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.\n\n### Dependencies\n- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)\n- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)\n\n## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit\n\nThe 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.\n\n## Bookkeeping\n\nA child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.",

After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo .ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/prompt.md:5:LAYER 3 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. Completeness work — fixes incomplete Relay spec compliance.

PROBLEM
cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.
.ddx/executions/20260503T031755-a62beb88/prompt.md:8:1. Node(id) at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go resolves Bead IDs (prefix "bead-") via the bead store scoped to context WorkingDir.
2. Node(id) resolves Execution run IDs (prefix "exec-" or whatever the run-id format is) via the executions store scoped to context WorkingDir.
3. Returns nil + appropriate error for IDs that don't match any known prefix.
4. Test: TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject — register two projects with overlapping bead IDs; query Node("bead-X") via project A's scoped GraphQL; assert returns project A's bead, not B's.
5. Test: TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject — same shape for execution runs.
6. Test: TestNode_ReturnsNilForUnknownPrefix — assert no panic, nil returned.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f4014bd5/events.jsonl:1:{"actor":"opus-reviewer","body":"Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.\n\n1. StateProvider interface split\n   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.\n\n2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)\n   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64(\"cursor:<slice-index>\"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.\n\n3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types\n   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.\n\n4. r.State == nil nil-check\n   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.\n\n5. node(id) prefix dispatch\n   Current: node(id) switches on \"node-\"/\"proj-\" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.\n\nNone of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.","created_at":"2026-04-14T22:44:50.987280017Z","kind":"note","source":"Stage 2.2 opus review shape concerns — propagated to 2.3","summary":"Five load-bearing decisions from Stage 2.2 review that Stage 2.3 should lock in before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7"}

Added BeadSnapshot + BeadDependencySnapshot full-copy in resolver_node.go:11-59 and state_graphql.go:61-87.\n4. Nil-checks — IGNORED. resolver_beads.go:11-13 and :32-34 both start with if r.State == nil early-return.\n5. node(id) prefix registry — IGNORED, AND node(id) does NOT handle bead- prefix.
